name: Wedding Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the stewing chicken in a large pot with 2 whole medium onions.
2. Add 2 cans of chicken broth and water to reach the desired height in the pot.
3. Season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
4. Simmer until the chicken falls off the bones.
5. Remove the chicken and let it cool; remove and chop the onions when cooled.
6. Clean the Endive and Escarole.
7. Simmer separately for about 10 minutes or until wilted; drain and squeeze dry.
8. Chop into small pieces and add to the chicken broth with celery, chopped onions, and carrots.
9. Debone the chicken and chop into small pieces.
10. Add to the pot.
11. Make tiny meatballs using the Meatball mix (ground pork and beef with breadcrumbs).
12. Simmer meatballs in water for a few minutes until set.
13. Add meatballs to the pot.
14. Cook all ingredients for about 3-4 hours.
15. Just before serving, cook 1 pound of small pasta and add to the soup.
16. Serve and enjoy! (Optional: sprinkle Parmesan cheese over the bowl of soup.)
17. Variation: Mix 4 beaten eggs with 1/4 cup grated Romano cheese and 1/4 cup bread crumbs; add towards the end of cooking time.
